title: Fit Check Call (40 min) between Jamie Riley
meeting_title: Fit Check Call (40 min) between Leonid Shvorob and Jamie Riley

transcript.text: Originally from Russia, but now I'm staying in Bali last three years.
Oh, okay.
How do you like it?
Yeah, it's pretty good.
Like fruits, like ocean.
And you from?
Good.
Okay, man.
So I received just your email today morning and you say the most challenge for you now is like finding new clients.
Right.
Can you tell me more about this?
Because you're like 20 years in the game and yeah, it's pretty good.
Well, the problem is there are a lot of people that I have to interact with that have, because their company spent a lot of money on investing into AI and automation and technology.
That they get into this group to where somebody that's got 20, they don't pay attention to who the hell's calling them, you know, and, and they believe in AI, they believe they've got it.
Uh, I had a company last year, uh, in November, they had 500 jobs open and the guy says, Oh, we got this.
Mm-hmm.
Come on.
It, you know, and, and they don't bother to look in a lot of cases, um, with who they're talking to now in fairness to them.
Uh, they get barraged by RPOs out of India, out of Philippines.
Uh, they get barraged with, uh, all kinds of stuff.
So they kind of, they, they kind of, uh, what's a good word?
They become numb.-hmm.
And when someone calls them that knows what the hell they're doing, they don't bother to dig deeper, I guess is the way to say it.
Yeah, so it's like inside your company or you're working with other agencies or someone who just give you this garbage leads?
Right now, it's me and one of my people.
We're doing the leads.
We're making phone calls.
sending emails.
Yeah, so in your website, you mentioned that you're working solo.
So I think it takes a lot of time to just doing calls and do this outreach.
Yeah, yeah.
I mean, I have a staff, but as far as doing the business development, me and I have one other lady doing it.
And, you know, we're together, we're doing maybe 100 calls a day.
Mm-hmm.
Yeah, no calls is the worst that I like to do.
Yeah.
When it's called calling, it's super annoying.
So what's usually, how many new clients with this volume you attracted for like four months or four quarters?
Over the last, we'll say the fourth quarter of last year, we got one new client and we filled those jobs.
Um, so, you know, it, the thing is 24 years in the game, I never had to market because I had an existing client list.
I had people that knew me and they'd say, Hey, we're having trouble getting this, you know, pharmacology filled, call this guy, you know, but now that shifted.
And a lot of the, it shifted and, and so I've got to do the business development stuff.
Mm-hmm.
So you mean it's,
So your referrals less often bring you new clients or what's like specific to where I've got existing clients.
Don't take me wrong.
I just got an email from one that I got to work on a very high level job for, but, but I want more because I want to build this up to the point that I have to bring on more recruiters.
I want to build this up to the point that in two to three years, if I decide to retire or walk away, it's big enough that I can sell it.
So you want, it's not dependence only on your inputs.
So you want, it was some system like outbound system.
So like other people who want to buy, they say, okay, this table, we can buy it.
And that's it.
Yeah.
I mean, I'm six, I'm 68 years old and I love what I do.
I've got the energy to do it.
I'm old I'm
I could keep doing it.
want to keep doing it.
But I also realized the day is going to come where, you know what, I just want to go home and hunt and fish the rest of my life.
I've already retired once from the military.
So it's just to build this as that I built and just decide to retire, walk away and shut it down is silly.
I'd rather build it and either hire someone to run it or sell it.
Yeah, totally makes sense because really, 20 years commitment, I think very few people can do this.
Yeah, there probably aren't that many recruiters in the pharma space that got over 20 years experience doing this.
Yeah, so like how many new clients you want to get in your pipeline for like next 90 days, let's say.
So it feel like, wow, this is good.
Next 90 days, five or six.
Now, realize that number may seem low, okay?
But you got to realize the type of things that we work on.
Yeah, I see you mentioned you work with Pfizer and like these big boys.
Yeah, well, it's the positions we work on are very intricate.
And they're not something that you can fill overnight, you know, like pharmacology and toxicology.
I mean, very deep science or engineering.
So if you gave me a job today, a director of pharmacology, I can guarantee you're going to have candidates within two weeks that fit the job.
I've been doing this long enough.
I can guarantee that, right?
But because of the interview process.
Mm
And they have to do presentations and all this stuff.
It's going to take 60 to 90 days to fill it.
So that's why I say five clients, if they've got two, three jobs apiece, that would be a good start.
And then I could ramp up to bring in on more recruiters, you know, so that's my initial game plan.
Yeah, that's a good plan.
But you said, like, for last quarter, you had one new client.
So, like, yeah, that's good.
So if nothing, like, changing, if only, like, relying on referrals and you're cold calling with your assistant, what do you think it's realistically?
Like, one, two new clients, three maximal, or?
I think this year, I think this year there's going to be a switch in the market.
Now, you're in Maryland.
this a good plan.
next time.
So you're close to the NIH and FDA, right?
So now I'm in Indonesia.
Oh, you're in Indonesia.
Oh, I'm sorry.
I'm sorry.
Well, here in the States, last year with the change of government, one of the big changes was the FDA and the NIH, which is the backbone of pharma and biotech, okay?
Those changes are now in place, and good things are happening.
There were a lot of layoffs last year, but there's a lot of companies coming to the States this year.
So that being said, I think last year was a client market that could be picky who they worked with.
I think this year, mid-year, it's going to be a candidate market where there's not going to be enough people to fill the jobs.
That's a good thing to me.
Of course.
I know if you know these niche-specific things.
things.
So I should trust your guts.
Yeah.
Tell me, please, like you might do executive search or what kind of placement?
Because you mentioned in your website, like you do staffing, you do recruiting, execs.
So what's your main focus?
Permanent positions, filling permanent positions.
So, for example, like you say, VP of pharmacology, like this, more high-level roles.
Yeah, anything dealing with, and there's a lot of different positions that go into these drug companies, anything that deals with drug development or drug discovery is a good way to encapsulate it.
Because, I mean, there's so many sciences, so many different types of engineering, and then there's a corporate side of regulatory compliance, all that stuff.
It's anybody that does anything to take a drug from a belief to the market, I do that.
stuff.
I've done them all.
Okay, I got you.
So it's like people with big brains who like do all this stuff.
Okay.
Okay.
Yeah.
So what kind of companies is it like, you know, if it's like Pfizer and super big boys, it's very difficult to target them.
So I dealt with Pfizer before I dealt with Novartis.
I don't want the big guns.
I want the startups to, you know, start up.
If they're a startup that is working on a drug all the way up to a midsize because they're serious.
You know, they got skin in the game.
You know, they, they, maybe they don't have a drug on the market yet, but they've raised capital where they've got millions of dollars invested.
They're serious about filling positions.
Those are the people I want to work with.
So like spin outs, like serious AB, like this kind of.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Small to medium size.
Companies may be anywhere from, you know, say 20 employees up to, you know, 2,500.
Okay, got you.
So let's say for next 90 days, I put you in front of like from 10 to 15 decision makers, if this kind of startups.
So would be it valuable for you or how many of this you could possibly close, like from your experience?
If in the next 90 days, I got in front of 15 companies that are, that are serious about hiring and developing compounds.
If I could, I've never had anybody I spoke, and this is fact, it's not bragging.
I'm not a braggadocious kind of guy.
Anybody that I've talked with that is...
If needed my help to fill a position, they've come on as a client.
And I have filled, I'd say, 85% to 90% of the jobs that I've been given.
That's great.
But what if, like, for example, they're already in job boards and you're competing with other recruitment agencies.
So what do you do in this situation?
Well, yeah, you're competing.
It's one, knowing what you're talking about.
So, right, if you have a job in pharmacometrics, pharmacology, whatever, and you're telling me what you want and I've got a job description, I know what that person does.
I know what they do because I've been doing it where a lot of younger recruiters don't know.
They go off of the job description solely.
Now, what I do, and again, as an example, if we were on the phone and you say, I need this pharmacologist, and I'll just use that because we've mentioned it.
This pharmacologist, and they're going to have these skill sets.
As soon as we get off the phone, I'm going to get started on the job.
I'm going to start reaching out to people I know.
I have a database of 178,000 people.
So I'm going to start reaching out to pharmacologists I know to see if they're interested.
Once I have a full understanding of what your need is, what you're willing to pay, you know, all that and more about the company, I'm going to start on it now.
And I'm going to make it my mission that you're going to have candidates to review within five to seven workdays.
Wow, that's crazy fast in this, like, not so easy roles.
Yeah, and, you know, because I've been doing it so long, I know a lot of people.
You know, and I'm not just going to send.
And, you know, a lot of recruiters, you open up a job to them and all of a sudden you get eight, 10 resumes, right?
I don't do that.
I look at it from a point.
If you're my hiring manager, I'm not a vendor.
I'm your partner.
It's my job to make your life easy.
So I'm only going to interview people and send people to you that I would hire to work for me.
Skills, personality, culture, you know, leadership, all of that.
Then I'm going to send them.
Now, at the same time, I'm not going to sit there if you're my hiring manager and say, I send you three people.
And if you don't move on those people in a short period, then I'm going to call you and say, what's the problem?
Because that's.
There's a process.
These people aren't sitting around, you know.
So it's a matter of process.
It's a matter of knowledge.
It's a matter of experience.
And my goal will be to get this job filled, get you interviewing people in a quick fashion to get that job filled.
Yeah, it makes sense.
It's good that you position yourself like as a partner.
That's also what I do with my clients, because I usually also like long-term relationship, not just like one time.
Exactly, exactly.
I want to, and you know, you may be a startup or a spinoff and only have 25 people working for you.
And you need this one job filled right now.
You may not need anything for three months, but I want to be the guy you call.
If you can't fill it yourself or you don't have the time to fill it.
I want to be the guy you call.
If it's one or two jobs, that's fine.
I want to be that guy.
And I have a client that I've been with since I got into this business 24 years.
They've been with me 24 years and they call me when it's critical and I'll fill three to five positions for them every year.
Yeah, that's great.
It looks like you don't have problems with fulfillment, only problem with finding new clients in this point.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
So happy to break down how I usually work in these situations.
What is the process?
Okay.
Okay.
So what I heard from now that you're really one of their like best experience in like biotech.
talk with Bancho like for last month.
And the problem is not fulfilling.
So the problem is that you don't know when somebody who know you 24 years call next time.
It's not like controllable.
And even you.
Doing outbound with yourself, with your system, think the most ROI usage of your time is talking with your network of bright brains, not just doing cold calls, right?
So yeah, we're in this point.
So what we can do is we change the process.
So we're not in passive position when we're waiting when somebody call you and give you like a new job.
So we will proactively reach out for companies who in this moment, right now, feeling the pain of hiring.
You mentioned that you worked before and it didn't work.
So we're not just blasting emails and LinkedIn, just pure volume.
So we're not doing this because I think you care about your reputation and it's better do less but more quality than just garbage leads.
So I always prefer quality.
Over.
Over.
Over.
So, but how we identify these companies?
So we monitor in the market and we see the signals that they will need your help before the job go to job boards when you need competing with other recruiters.
So, sometimes if they got funding, new series like ABC, so they have money, they need to hire, or if the leadership change, for example, they hire a new CEO and CEO want to bring new team.
So, in exact same moment, we make introductions and other signals like one company acquire other company and so on.
So, in exact same moment, when we see the signal, we introduce you to these decision makers, to these high-end managers.
So, we position you as a partner, not a vendor, who can for like five, seven days fill the role.
And we qualify them.
Do they really need it right now?
Now, do they have money?
Do they have urgency?
And only if this factor aligns, we make introduction.
So you're not wasting time.
You're just talking with people who really need it right now.
And as you say, when you're talking, it means like 80-90% you will help these clients to find their roles they need.
Does that sound valuable or you have some questions?
What system do you use to reach these hiring managers?
So, as I say, we are monitoring the signals.
So, for example, we're monitoring like LinkedIn, other databases.
So, when we got this, and then we're reaching out like emails or LinkedIn.
So, the question is not how we're reaching out to them, in what time and for what reason we do this.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
And have you worked with staffing and recruiting companies in the States before?
Yeah, of course.
Now I'm targeting only U.S.
and recruitment.
We're working with different clients.
Maybe you check on my website.
If you want, I will send you some case studies that we've done if you need it.
Okay.
And what kind of return have your previous clients seen?
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
So let's say it's a defensive process, but for like 90 days, usually I'm not doing like one month or like two months because usually it takes time to do it like quality.
And after this, months after months, when I continue working with clients, it's just getting better, better, and better.
But sometimes maybe it takes some initial time.
So for 90 days, usually if it's more like...
General roles, like staffing, more like staff, not like CEOs or something, it can be 10 to 15 introductions.
So from this, usually, if the sales process not sucks, they close like two or three people.
So usually, like, I didn't ask you, but I think, like, what's usually fee in your space, like 30K, 50K?
Depending on the position between 30 and 60.
Yeah, so let's say it's, like, even one placement for 90 days, yeah?
It's already will be return on investment, like 10X.
So if you want, I can say how we structure things, or if you have any question, feel free to ask.
Yeah, well, yeah, I would like to read how you structure things, read more about what, how you do what you do.
What is the cost?
Mm-hmm.
Yeah, good question.
It's 3K per month for 90 days is 9K.
Okay.
And what is, is there a guarantee with that or how does that work?
Yes, I guarantee.
So you will have at least 10 qualified introductions for the next 90 days.
So we define in there what is qualified, but usually is they hiring right now, they like have urgency, they have money.
So this is like a criteria.
And are you using just email?
Are you using phone calls?
What are you using?
So definitely how I reach out to you, right?
Using emails, if needs, I can call to make things faster.
But usually in this kind of roles, they don't have so many emails in their inbox.
So usually they're replying pretty fast.
But if needed, of course, we're doing everything to...
Bye.
Improve the conversion.
Okay.
Yeah.
Send me some information on what you're just talking about and your cost breakdown and all that stuff.
Send me that because we've had several conversations with people that do business development.
I'd rather have someone else doing it than me.
I'd rather spend my time recruiting.
So, yeah.
Send me that and let me look at it and let's circle back and get together.
Okay.
So, okay.
I will send you more info.
guess tomorrow it's already like zero, zero, but let's schedule like when you will be free this week.
It's like Thursday would be able in like Saturday.
I won't be in here Saturday.
I
Next Tuesday.
Tuesday.
Okay.
So Tuesday, same time?
Yeah.
Okay, perfect.
So it's not a lot of details, so it's pretty straightforward as I mentioned, but I sell you all info, so you can just share it with your team or something.
And next Tuesday, do we need to invite someone on, like, next call when I put you through the, like, proposal and so on, or you're the final?
No, it's just me.
Okay.
That's great.
All right.
Yeah, next Tuesday works.
Okay, next Tuesday, 10 a.m.
on your time.
I will send you an invitation, like, tonight.
Okay?
Okay.
All right.
Thank you.
Yeah.
Thank you.
Really was enjoying talking with you.
Yes, sir.
Thank you much.
Yeah.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[To explore a partnership for client acquisition.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=20.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Problem:** Jamie Riley (24 yrs exp) needs a new client acquisition system. Referrals are declining, and cold calls (\~100/day) yielded only one new client last quarter, stalling growth.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=183.0)
  - [**Goal:** Secure 5–6 new clients in 90 days to scale the business for a future sale.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=346.0)
  - [**Proposed Solution:** SystemHustle will use market signals (e.g., funding rounds) to proactively introduce Riley to qualified decision-makers at target companies (startups, mid-size).](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=1036.0)
  - [**Offer:** A 90-day engagement for $9k ($3k/mo), guaranteeing 10 qualified introductions.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=1310.0)

## Topics

### Client Acquisition Challenge

  - [**Core Problem:** Referrals, the primary client source for 24 years, are declining.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=183.0)
  - [**Ineffective Outreach:** Current efforts (cold calls, emails) are ineffective.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=183.0)
      - [**Volume:** \~100 calls/day by Riley and one assistant.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)
      - [**Result (Q4 2025):** One new client.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=183.0)
  - [**Market Noise:** Decision-makers are "numb" to outreach from low-quality RPOs, causing them to dismiss even experienced recruiters.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=40.0)

### Business Goals & Market Outlook

  - [**90-Day Goal:** Secure 5–6 new clients.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=346.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This number provides enough volume to hire more recruiters and begin scaling.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=346.0)
  - [**Long-Term Vision:** Build a scalable business for a future sale or to be run by another leader.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=250.0)
  - [**Market Prediction:** The market will shift from a client-led to a candidate-led market mid-year, increasing demand for recruiting services.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=468.0)

### Proposed Solution: Proactive Lead Generation

  - [**Strategy:** Proactively identify hiring needs before they are advertised, avoiding competition.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)
  - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=1036.0)
    1.  [**Monitor Market Signals:** Track events indicating imminent hiring (e.g., funding rounds, leadership changes, M\&A).](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)
    2.  [**Qualify Leads:** Vet prospects for urgency, budget, and decision-maker status.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=1136.0)
    3.  [**Make Introductions:** Connect Riley only with qualified decision-makers.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=1118.0)
  - [**Method:** Primarily email, with phone calls as needed to accelerate conversions.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=1356.0)

### Offer & Guarantee

  - [**Cost:** $9,000 for a 90-day engagement ($3,000/month).](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=1310.0)
  - [**Guarantee:** 10 qualified introductions within 90 days.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=1327.0)
      - [**Definition:** Prospects actively hiring, with budget, urgency, and decision-making authority.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=1327.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Leonid Shvorob:** Send Riley a proposal with the process, cost, and guarantee.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=1384.0)
  - [**Jamie Riley:** Review the proposal.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=1384.0)
  - [**Both:** Meet on **Tuesday, January 27, at 10:00 AM (Riley's time)** to discuss.](https://fathom.video/share/YU4-MeP1KEC65oNs29DKbyyD2TgjMPyK?tab=summary&timestamp=1421.0)

action_items.description: Email Jamie proposal + pricing/guarantee/process + case studies; request review before Tue call
Send Jamie calendar invite for Tue 10 a.m. (Jamie's time)
